name: Jesus blesses the little children
notes: A threefold synoptic parallel in which children are brought to Jesus to be touched and blessed. Matthew, Mark, and Luke share the core: the bringing of the little children (Luke specifies infants), the disciples' rebuke, the Lord's word to suffer them to come and forbid them not, and the saying that of such is the kingdom of God (Matthew: of heaven). Mark and Luke alone preserve the further saying that whosoever will not receive the kingdom as a little child shall in no wise enter therein. The accounts give complementary vantages on the touching and blessing: Matthew tells that he laid his hands on them, while Mark adds the fuller picture that he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them. Mark also notes that Jesus was much displeased at the rebuke, a detail left as a matter for further study where the others are silent.
